name: Enoura Observatory
locationJa: 神奈川県小田原市江之浦362-1
description: A complex conceived over 20 years by Sugimoto, opened in 2017. Features a 100-meter solstice gallery, Noh stage, glass stage, tea houses, and stone garden — all oriented to mark the winter solstice sunrise.

name: Go'o Shrine (Art House Project)
description: Renovation of a 17th-century Shinto shrine as part of Benesse's Art House Project. Sugimoto integrated a subterranean glass staircase and underground chamber, connecting the sacred ground above to an ancient void below.

name: Coffin of Light
location: Benesse House Park, Naoshima, Kagawa, Japan
description: Permanent outdoor installation at Benesse House Park. A glass sarcophagus-like form filled with light, exploring the boundary between life and death through luminescence.

name: Concept of Moss
location: Benesse House Park, Naoshima, Kagawa, Japan
description: Permanent outdoor sculpture at Benesse House Park. A large stone form evoking geological deep time, placed in dialogue with the landscape of the Seto Inland Sea.

name: Glass Tea House "Mondrian"
location: Benesse House Park, Naoshima, Kagawa, Japan
description: A fully transparent tea house constructed from optical glass, originally exhibited at Venice and Versailles. Now permanently installed at Benesse House Park, where tea ceremonies are offered to visitors.

name: Hiroshi Sugimoto Gallery: Time Corridors
nameJa: 杉本博司ギャラリー 時間の回廊
location: Benesse House Park, Naoshima, Kagawa, Japan
description: Opened in 2022, this dedicated gallery was renovated by New Material Research Laboratory. It displays Sugimoto's photographs, sculptures, and design objects as a unified permanent installation, with tea service included.

name: MOA Museum of Art (Renovation)
nameJa: MOA美術館（リノベーション）
description: New Material Research Laboratory redesigned the entrance hall, main lobby, and key exhibition spaces of this mountainside museum overlooking Sagami Bay, reopened in 2017 after a major renovation.

name: 究竟頂 / Mathematical Model 013
nameJa: 究竟頂（くっきょうちょう）
location: Oak Omotesando, 3-6-1 Kitaaoyama, Minato, Tokyo, Japan
locationJa: 東京都港区北青山3-6-1 オーク表参道 2Fエントランスホール
description: A 500-ton granite hall evoking the topmost chamber of Kinkaku-ji, housing a 6-meter-long suspended mathematical model (a surface of revolution tapering to 5mm at its tip). The sculpture hangs inverted from the ceiling at Sugimoto's own suggestion.

name: SUN DIAL
location: Otemachi Place, 2-3-1 Otemachi, Chiyoda, Tokyo, Japan
locationJa: 東京都千代田区大手町2-3-1 大手町プレイス 1F西ゲート南
description: A 12-meter-tall aluminum sculpture derived from a cubic mathematical function, functioning simultaneously as a sundial. Its shadow marks solar noon at the equinoxes, connecting the office district to deep astronomical time.

name: Point of Infinity
nameJa: ポイント・オブ・インフィニティ
location: Yerba Buena Island, San Francisco, California, USA
locationJa: ヤーバ・ブエナ島、サンフランシスコ、カリフォルニア州
description: A stainless steel sculpture approximately 21 meters tall, installed on a hilltop overlooking San Francisco Bay. Also functions as a sundial. Sugimoto's first large-scale public sculpture in the United States.

name: Hirshhorn Museum Lobby Redesign
nameJa: ハーシュホーン美術館 ロビー改修
location: Hirshhorn Museum, Independence Ave SW & 7th St SW, Washington, DC, USA
locationJa: ハーシュホーン美術館、ワシントンD.C.
description: A permanent redesign of the Hirshhorn Museum's lobby, the first major renovation in over 40 years. Features a table made from a 700-year-old nutmeg root and a brass café counter, exploring the circle as a unifying form between nature and artifice.

name: Hirshhorn Sculpture Garden Redesign
nameJa: ハーシュホーン彫刻庭園 改修
location: Hirshhorn Museum, Independence Ave SW & 7th St SW, Washington, DC, USA
locationJa: ハーシュホーン美術館、ワシントンD.C.
description: A comprehensive redesign of the Hirshhorn's outdoor sculpture garden — the first in over 40 years — led by Sugimoto. Approved by national arts commissions and completed in 2024–2025. Note: confirm current open status before visiting.

name: Mathematical Model 012
location: Château La Coste, 2750 Route de La Cride, Le Puy-Sainte-Réparade, Provence, France
locationJa: シャトー・ラ・コスト、プロヴァンス、フランス
description: A permanent sculpture based on a mathematical model of a surface of revolution with constant negative curvature, placed on a shallow reflecting pool in the vineyard's art trail alongside works by Tadao Ando, Louise Bourgeois, and others.

name: Confession of Zero
location: Castello di Ama, Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Tuscany, Italy
locationJa: カステッロ・ディ・アマ、キャンティ、トスカーナ、イタリア
description: A permanent installation inside an 18th-century chapel at the winery. Mathematical model sculptures in marble and stainless steel are placed within the sacred space, meditating on zero as both nothingness and origin.

name: Japan Society Atrium Garden
nameJa: ジャパン・ソサエティー アトリウム・ガーデン
location: Japan Society, 333 East 47th Street, New York, NY, USA
locationJa: ジャパン・ソサエティー、ニューヨーク
description: Renovation of the Japan Society's atrium and interior garden by New Material Research Laboratory. A serene stone garden and water feature transform the building's central space, bringing a meditative Japanese sensibility to Midtown Manhattan.

name: Time Exposed
nameJa: タイム・エクスポーズド
location: Benesse House Museum, Naoshima, Kagawa, Japan
locationJa: 香川県直島町 ベネッセハウスミュージアム
description: A permanent installation of Sugimoto's 'Seascapes' series photographs at Benesse House Museum. The works are displayed as a unified site-specific presentation, in dialogue with the actual sea view visible through the museum's windows.
